archetype_reasoning: This trader demonstrates extraordinary accuracy in two specific domains — Russia_UKR (86.0%, 758 wins) and Trump_US (85.5%, 248 wins) — while catastrophically failing outside them with only 12.6% accuracy in 'Other' (49 wins, 339 losses). The sharp domain split across only 4 distinct geo markets confirms this is a concentrated specialist, not a generalist. The directionality of 0.786 further supports directional conviction within familiar territory.

domain_summary: This trader has genuine, high-confidence edge in Russia-Ukraine and Trump/US political markets with ~86% win rates across 1,100+ trades. Outside these two domains, their accuracy collapses to near-random (actually sharply below random), suggesting zero transferable edge and possible overconfidence.

Any signal from this trader in Other domains should be actively discounted or used as a contra-indicator given the 12.6% accuracy rate on 388 resolved trades — this is not noise, it is systematic misjudgment outside core domains.

summary: This is a genuinely elite trader within a narrow but deep specialisation: Russia_UKR and Trump_US markets where their 85-86% win rates across 1,000+ trades represent exceptional, non-trivial forecasting edge. However, the 'Other' domain performance (12.6% accuracy, 339 losses) is one of the clearest domain-blindspot patterns in the dataset — trades outside core domains should be treated as contra-indicators. The recent Harris dropout loss series (15 consecutive losses, entries at 0.67-0.73) reveals a dangerous sunk-cost tendency when conviction overrides information. Signal value is HIGH but strictly domain-gated: copy Russia_UKR and Trump_US calls, fade everything else.
